label: Provenance, citations & evidence tracking

node.label: Provenance, citations & evidence tracking
node.definition: Provenance and citation tracking attach claims to source identifiers, retrieved passages, transformations, timestamps, and evidence paths.
node.why_it_matters: The role of Provenance, citations & evidence tracking is to connect External retrieval & ranking, Databases & information retrieval to downstream capabilities including Neural search, RAG & question answering, Memory lifecycle, forgetting & consolidation, Content credentials, signing & provenance.
examples: substrate for Neural search, RAG & question answering
substrate for Memory lifecycle, forgetting & consolidation
substrate for Content credentials, signing & provenance
limitations: Stored or retrieved information can be stale, private, contradictory, inaccessible, or incorrectly ranked at the moment it is needed. Its direct dependencies include External retrieval & ranking, Databases & information retrieval.

dependencies.rationale: Provenance, citations & evidence tracking uses External retrieval & ranking as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Provenance, citations & evidence tracking uses Databases & information retrieval as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Provenance, citations & evidence tracking stores, selects, or obtains information through Retrieval-augmented generation.
